what_it_does: A live integration probe for Session Fabric R1 that connects to a local session supervisor, spawns a real Claude session, and exercises five operational legs: registry flag propagation (pre-assigned session_id and appended system prompt), slash command dispatch, genuine tool-use observation, honest /rewind recording, and full raw-stream capture with declared-event coverage. It watches the supervisor's /watch ndjson endpoint, writes events to a capture file, and prints a neutral OBSERVED/NOT-OBSERVED/RECORDED proof summary.
observations: Implements a Watcher thread that tees /watch ndjson into memory and a capture file for the probe's lifetime.
Uses a mark()/wait_for(since=mark()) cursor so each leg only matches events emitted after its own start.
Pre-assigns a UUID session_id and an appended system-prompt rule, then checks whether the session's system/init echoes them back.
Selects a fallback slash command (/usage) if /context is not listed in the init slash_commands field.
Records /rewind output verbatim without claiming it performs a headless file rewind, citing Atlas documentation that /rewind is interactive.
Supports a --keep flag to leave sessions alive and a stub plumbing mode via COMPANY_CLAUDE_BIN.
Returns exit code 0 only when every proof leg is observed/recorded.
standouts: Explicit 'never green-paints' design: every leg reports OBSERVED, NOT-OBSERVED, or RECORDED with captured evidence paths.
The mark/wait_for cursor is a deliberate safeguard against matching stale events from previous turns.
Honest handling of /rewind limitations distinguishes observed behavior from unsupported headless file rewind.
conventions: Follows the repo's module docstring convention with usage, prerequisites, and honest caveats.
Uses Python type hints and __future__ annotations.
Communicates with the supervisor over plain HTTP using urllib.request and JSON bodies.
Daemon thread for background event consumption.
concerns: Hardcoded default supervisor port 8771 and no authentication on the HTTP API.
Watcher appends to the same capture file indefinitely and uses long blocking timeouts (300s for /watch, 240s for turn done).
If the probe raises before the final teardown block, the watcher thread and spawned session may not be cleaned up.
req() swallows HTTP errors by returning status/body rather than raising, so callers must explicitly check codes.
notes: This is the lead-only live probe that runs real Claude sessions to collect neutral evidence for the R1 fabric spec lines.
questions: What exact schema do /watch declared events use for tool_use blocks and system/init fields?
How does the supervisor behave when COMPANY_CLAUDE_BIN points to a stub binary?
Is the capture directory .build/fabric-probe expected to be gitignored or treated as a build artifact?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is a runnable, evidence-collecting live probe for R1 with no pre-claimed successes.
intention_signals: The file is trying to turn the R1 spec lines into reproducible, evidence-backed observations against a live supervisor, while explicitly refusing to report false positives.
novelty: It combines real Claude session spawning with a self-refreshing event cursor and an explicit RECORDED category for operations known to be interactive/unsupported headlessly.
contribution: A runnable, file-local integration probe plus a reusable /watch event watcher that captures raw stream evidence and reports neutral observation status for downstream ledger queries.
summary_for_embedding: ops/fabric_live_probe_r1.py is a live integration probe that spawns real Claude sessions through a local supervisor, exercises slash commands, tool use, registry flags, and /rewind, captures the /watch event stream, and emits a neutral OBSERVED/NOT-OBSERVED proof summary.
intention_for_embedding: To provide honest, evidence-based proof-of-operation for Session Fabric R1 spec lines without green-painting results.

symbols.does: Custom exception used to signal unrecoverable probe failures.
symbols.description: A thin Exception subclass raised when the probe encounters a blocking error, such as a session closing unexpectedly or failing to become idle within the timeout.

symbols.does: Sends JSON HTTP requests to the supervisor and returns status plus parsed body.
symbols.description: A small urllib.request wrapper that builds a JSON request, handles HTTPError by returning the error code and body, and returns a (status, json) tuple. It does not raise on non-2xx responses.

symbols.does: Background thread that consumes a session's /watch ndjson stream and stores events in memory plus a file.
symbols.description: Inherits from threading.Thread and runs as a daemon. It opens the supervisor's /watch endpoint for a given session, appends each line to a capture file, and keeps a shared in-memory list of parsed JSON events for wait_for queries.

symbols.does: Initializes the watcher with supervisor base URL, session id, and capture file path.
symbols.description: Sets up the event list, lock, stop event, and stores the base URL, session id, and path used by run().

symbols.does: Streams /watch ndjson and appends events to the capture file and in-memory list until stopped.
symbols.description: Opens the watch endpoint, reads lines, writes them to the capture file with flush, parses JSON, and appends parsed events under a lock. It exits on exceptions or when the stop event is set.

symbols.does: Signals the watcher thread to stop reading the stream.
symbols.description: Sets the internal stop event so that run() breaks out of its read loop on the next iteration.

symbols.does: Returns the current length of the event list as a cursor.
symbols.description: Provides a snapshot index so that wait_for(since=mark()) only considers events emitted after the mark, preventing stale matches from earlier turns.

symbols.does: Polls events emitted after a cursor for a predicate match within a timeout.
symbols.description: Scans the in-memory event list starting at the given index, returns the first event satisfying the predicate, or None after the timeout expires. It sleeps in short intervals while holding the lock minimally.

symbols.does: Collects render_key values from all events of type 'declared'.
symbols.description: Returns a list of render_key strings extracted from declared events seen so far, used to summarize declaration coverage and detect undeclared drops.

symbols.does: Polls /sessions until the target session reaches the 'idle' state.
symbols.description: Repeatedly queries the supervisor's session list, returning the session record once its state is idle, or raising ProbeFail if it becomes closed or the timeout expires.

symbols.does: Runs the full R1 live probe and prints a neutral proof summary.
symbols.description: Parses arguments, checks supervisor health, spawns a session with registry flags, starts a Watcher, then executes the five legs: R1.3 flag echo and appended prompt, R1.1a slash command, R1.1b tool use, R1.1c /rewind recording, and R1.2 live capture coverage. It tears down the session unless --keep is set and exits 0 only when every leg is observed or recorded.
